segments.text: Luxury leather means chemicals, waste,
and animal cruelty. But what if the best
leather does not require animals at all?
Brands today are turning everyday food
waste into high-end leather
alternatives. Let me show you the three
wildest ones. First, we have apple
leather made from leftover apple peels
from juice factories. They grind, dry,
and turn the waste into soft, flexible
leather, which is then used in sneakers
and bags. But still, this is very less
produced as compared to coconut leather
because billions of coconut husks are
thrown every single year. A startup
thought why not turn this waste into a
breathable, antibacterial, fully
biodegradable leather. It is strong,
lightweight and made from what we
normally dump. Now the one which even
shocked the big brands, it is Pineetex.
It comes from [music] pineapple leaf
fibers which are usually burned as a
waste. These fibers are pressed into
durable sheets which are used by brands
like Hugo Boss. It's tough, water
resistant and totally zero waste. So
what do you prefer plant or animal
leather?
